meta.title: Should Adobe deprecate Photoshop's UI within 3 years?
meta.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title:
Should Adobe deprecate
Photoshop's UI within 3 years?
slides.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title: The aggressive deprecation path survives <15% of plausible scenarios; ship a segmented dual-SKU instead
slides.action: Do NOT deprecate the Photoshop UI. Ship Photoshop Pro (UI + 10-year LTS) alongside Photoshop Agentic (Firefly AI Assistant native), with binding public reversal triggers.

fields.label: CONFIDENCE
fields.value: HIGH on do-not-deprecate · MEDIUM-HIGH on segmented+LTS structure · MEDIUM on board willingness to publish reversal triggers

fields.label: DEPENDS ON
fields.value: Board willing to publish binding reversal triggers · LTS commitment credible to pros · Segments are separable · Adobe execution amortizes dual-SKU R&D

fields.label: MONITOR
fields.value: Firefly AI Assistant beta NPS · Acrobat UI backlash trajectory · OpenAI/Anthropic creative product launches · Figma post-IPO M&A · Enterprise ELA renewal cohort

fields.label: PRE-MORTEM
fields.value: h6 without binding triggers (P=0.30) · Foundation model plateau (P=0.25) · Pro community rejects LTS as marketing-only (P=0.20) · Internal resourcing split degrades both SKUs (P=0.55)

fields.label: DEEPEST DISAGREEMENT
fields.value: Optimist: 55% first-mover platform lock compounds. Pessimist: 75% reversal-impossible franchise damage. Disagreement IS the data.

fields.label: REVIEW TRIGGER
fields.value: Firefly AI Assistant beta NPS <40 · OpenAI/Anthropic ships native creative · Adobe board refuses public reversal triggers · Q2 FY26 Digital Media growth <10%

slides.source: Source: Decision Brief (full mode, 2 iterations, council 5/5 endorsement on h6) · [G15], [C3:council], [C4:pessimist], [C17:council]

slides.lead_question: A 5-persona council, 6 competing hypotheses, two iterations to convergence, and 12 adversarial stress scenarios — what survived?

slides.title: The analysis explored 6 hypotheses across 2 iterations of a 5-persona council, stress-tested by 12 adversarial scenarios
bullets: 5 personas spawned as separate subagents — Optimist, Pessimist, Competitor, Regulator, Customer — each with independent context
6 hypotheses generated, ranging from aggressive deprecation (h1) to additive Adobe-actual strategy (h3) to the synthesis winner (h6 segmented dual-SKU + LTS + binding reversal triggers)
2 iterations — convergence reached at iteration 2 with 78% assumption stability, 1 productive contradiction remaining
Adversary phase generated 12 stress scenarios across worst-cases, black swans, and irrational actors
Sensitivity analysis on 10 key assumptions — 3 high-sensitivity, 5 medium, 2 low
Real-time anchor: Adobe announced Firefly AI Assistant as additive on April 15, 2026 [G15] — three days before this analysis. The announced strategy is h3, the recommendation upgrades to h6.
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief Convergence Log; engine-protocol.md

slides.title: Six hypotheses surfaced; only h6 survived 5/5 council endorsement and adversarial stress
rows: Agentic is terminal paradigm · 36-month parity · Foundation labs enter creative
Muscle-memory switching cost real · Narrative damage sticky · Enterprise procurement revolts
Hybrid additive dominates (Adobe's actual April 2026 strategy [G15])
Both modes coexist · Market rewards additive · AI Assistant quality holds
Foundation labs enter creative · Category narrative matters · Translates to valuation
Segments separable · Dual tier doesn't confuse · Pricing power preserved
Dual SKU separable · LTS credible · Reversal triggers enforceable

slides.title: Plotted on reversibility × franchise risk, h6 sits in the manageable quadrant; h1 sits where every adversarial scenario amplifies

items.label: h1
items.legend: Aggressive deprecation — irreversible, catastrophic if wrong (joint P 12-20%)

items.label: h2
items.legend: User revolt — risk path of h1, same quadrant

items.label: h3
items.legend: Hybrid additive — Adobe's actual April 2026 strategy [G15]

items.label: h6
items.legend: Segmented + LTS + binding reversal triggers — captures growth while preserving optionality

slides.source: Source: Synthesis from Decision Brief — bubble size = council_agreement × probability of dominance

slides.lead_question: Where the personas converged, where they diverged, and what the disagreement itself reveals about the decision.

slides.title: The strongest signals point to franchise risk under aggressive paths and quiet compounding under additive

effects.label: Pro segment churn 18-28% IF aggressive deprecation
AI Assistant captures 80M+ freemium MAUs at 2-3x
Execution risk: 30-yr feature surface in 36 months
h6 binding reversal triggers eliminate one-way-door risk
Acrobat UI precedent escalates to Photoshop
Firefly flywheel compounds via session data
h6 dual SKU separates cleanly (5/5 council)

commentary_lines: Top 12 effects ranked by council_agreement × probability
Bar color = council agreement (navy = 5/5, fading to pale = 1/5)
Black bracket = persona disagreement range — width is the uncertainty signal
Two of the three highest-agreement effects (5/5) are downside under aggressive paths
h6 effects (last row) emerge in iteration 2 with full council endorsement
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Effects Map (top 15 by council_agreement × probability) · [C7:council], [C10:council], [C16:council]

slides.title: The deepest disagreement — competitive urgency vs franchise preservation — IS the data
left_items: Won on structural depth — EU AI Act obligations, Firefly training-data suit risk, fiduciary / Delaware business judgment rule, ADA Section 508 implications of chat-only UI
Surfaced TWO of the alternatives that synthesized into h6: dual-SKU LTS + binding reversal triggers
Identified h6 as protected under business judgment rule — reversibility insulates the board even if strategy fails
Compliance moat (AI Act + copyright + enterprise DPA) buys 18-24 months regardless of UI strategy
right_items: Optimist + Competitor: narrow window, first-mover lock compounds (P=0.55 [C2:optimist])
Pessimist + Regulator + Customer: catastrophic franchise downside under forced transition (P=0.75 [C4:pessimist] reversal impossible)
Disagreement range on foundation_labs_enter_creative: 0.30-0.70 [C3:council] — the disagreement IS the data
Optimist was weakest (#5 peer rank) — joint-probability inflation flagged by 4/4 reviewers
BLIND SPOT caught: 4/5 personas missed agentic inference COGS — ~10-50× per session, material at 41M subs

slides.title: Regulator scored highest on structural depth and counter-factual rigor; Optimist's narrow ranges drove its #5 peer rank
commentary_lines: Each persona scored 1-5 across five rigor dimensions, derived from peer-review and critique outputs
Regulator's structural depth surfaced both alternatives that synthesized into h6 — dual-SKU LTS and binding reversal triggers
Optimist's narrow range and weak counter-factual depth flagged in 4/4 reviews — joint-probability inflation pattern
Pessimist + Regulator together cover the full adversarial surface; their alignment on irreversibility risk drove the h6 design
Customer's lower adversarial coverage is structural — the role focuses on adoption value, not threats
slides.source: Source: Synthesized from Decision Brief — Council Dynamics + Critique outputs

slides.title: Twelve adversarial scenarios — eight push toward kill-aggressive or segmented; only h6's reversal triggers neutralize all of them

items: Pro community organizes mass migration via plugin APIs (P=0.20)
EU AI Act enforcement hits Firefly mid-transition (P=0.20)
Senior ML talent departs during stock-compression trough (P=0.30)
Apple ships Vision Pro agentic Final Cut + Photos Dec 2027 (P=0.15)
Figma acquires Runway or Midjourney with post-IPO war chest (P=0.15)
CEO Narayen retires mid-transition; successor reverses (P=0.10)
China bans Creative Cloud during trade escalation; $2-3B revenue (P=0.10)
F500 flagship client publicly abandons Adobe; narrative contagion (P=0.15)
Viral 'Adobe killed Photoshop' video from top creator (P=0.20)
Activist investor takes 5% stake demanding reversal during trough (P=0.15)
Board member leaks internal concerns; stock further compresses (P=0.08)

slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Adversarial Scenarios · [C23:adversary] through [C35:adversary]

slides.title: Three high-sensitivity assumptions are load-bearing — if any one fails, aggressive deprecation becomes indefensible

assumptions.label: Adobe ships agentic parity in 36 months
Agentic is terminal UI paradigm for creative
Adobe board willing to publish binding reversal triggers
Market rewards additive strategy for cash-cow incumbents
Enterprise procurement revolts against forced UI transition
Muscle-memory switching cost real for 20+ year pros
Narrative damage from deprecation announcement is sticky
LTS commitment on Pro SKU is credible (not marketing-only)

commentary_lines: Bar length = sensitivity weight × number of effects impacted
Dark-blue bar = HIGH sensitivity, lighter = MEDIUM
All four HIGH-sensitivity assumptions are external — Adobe controls none directly
h6's binding reversal triggers convert assumption-failure from catastrophic to recoverable
If 36-month parity slips (>60% probability per [C1:council]), aggressive deprecation guarantees the partial-agentic awkward state — exactly what h6 is structured to prevent
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Key Assumptions · sensitivity per effects-impacted × HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW weight

slides.lead_question: Six checkpoints with binding reversal triggers — every gate has a kill criterion before the next commitment.

slides.title: The recommendation maps to a six-checkpoint roadmap with clear kill criteria at each stage
slides.subtitle: Every checkpoint has a binding reversal trigger; failure at any gate triggers either reversal or extension of LTS
rows: Publish Pro LTS commitment + Agentic SKU roadmap
Public commitment with binding reversal triggers
Board refuses to publish → fall back to pure h3
Firefly AI Assistant GA across CC apps
Ship slip > 2 quarters → narrative damage risk
Agentic SKU builds; Pro SKU continues UI investment
Pro churn > 5% OR Agentic ARPU < $10 → reversal
OpenAI/Anthropic creative products shipped or not
Pro churn > 8% → reversal; <3% + strong agentic → continue
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Appendix A: Decision Timeline · binding reversal triggers per [C17:council]
